openmeter/subscription/addon/diff.purpose: Pure in-memory spec mutation library converting a SubscriptionAddon into a bidirectional Apply/Restore transform on SubscriptionSpec. Its sole constraint is invertibility: Apply followed by Restore must yield the original spec with no DB or service calls.

patterns.name: Diffable interface as the unit of work
patterns.description: Every spec mutation is a Diffable{GetApplies() AppliesToSpec, GetRestores() AppliesToSpec}; both return an AppliesToSpec passed to spec.Apply(). Never call a repo or domain service inside a Diffable.
patterns.example: diffable, err := addondiff.GetDiffableFromAddon(subView, subsAdd); spec.Apply(diffable.GetApplies(), subscription.ApplyContext{CurrentTime: now})

patterns.name: Gap-based item insertion in apply.go
patterns.description: apply.go tracks coverage gaps ([]timeutil.OpenPeriod) starting from the full addon cadence, subtracts each existing item's intersection, then creates new SubscriptionItemSpec for remaining gaps; overlapping items are split into difference + intersection fragments.
patterns.example: gaps := []timeutil.OpenPeriod{*addInPhase}; for _, item := range items { nGaps = append(nGaps, g.Difference(itemPer)...) }

patterns.name: Quantity application asymmetry
patterns.description: For existing items within the addon cadence, rc.Apply is called Quantity times. For gap-created items (which start from the RateCard base) it is called Quantity-1 times because the RateCard already represents one application.
patterns.example: for range d.addon.Quantity { rc.Apply(inst.RateCard, inst.Annotations) } // existing item; gap item uses Quantity-1

patterns.name: Relative cadence via ISODuration offsets
patterns.description: setItemRelativeCadence converts absolute timestamps to ISO 8601 durations relative to phase start via datetime.ISODurationBetween, stored as ActiveFrom/ToOverrideRelativeToPhaseStart. Never store absolute times in cadence overrides.
patterns.example: diff := datetime.ISODurationBetween(phaseCadence.ActiveFrom, *target.From); item.ActiveFromOverrideRelativeToPhaseStart = &diff

patterns.name: Restore merges adjacent equal items
patterns.description: restore.go undoes addon effects per-item via AddonRateCard.Restore, deletes items where zeroRateCardCheck.CanDelete() is true, then merges consecutive items with equal RateCard + Annotations + BillingBehaviorOverride whose cadences are adjacent.
patterns.example: canMerge := targetItem.RateCard.Equal(testItem.RateCard) && reflect.DeepEqual(targetItem.Annotations, testItem.Annotations) && targetCadence.ActiveTo.Equal(testCadence.ActiveFrom)

patterns.name: Zero rate-card deletion guard
patterns.description: zeroRateCardCheck (zeroratecard.go) decides if a restored item can be deleted by inspecting FlatPrice amount, EntitlementTemplate type/value, and OwnerSubSystem annotations. Only if all checks pass is the item removed.
patterns.example: chk := zeroRateCardCheck{itemAnnotations: item.Annotations, rc: target}; if chk.CanDelete() { rmIdxs = append(rmIdxs, idx) }

key_file_guides.role: Defines the Diffable interface and someDiffable helper; the two-method contract (GetApplies/GetRestores) is the only public API shape.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never add I/O here — Diffable is a pure value type with no side-effects.

key_file_guides.role: GetDiffableFromAddon converts a SubscriptionAddon into a Diffable by mapping instances to per-instance diffables aggregated via NewAggregateAppliesToSpec.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Returns nil, nil when the addon has no instances — callers must check for a nil Diffable before spec.Apply.

key_file_guides.role: Core apply algorithm: iterates phases in addon cadence range, manages gaps, splits items, applies rc.Apply() the correct number of times.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Quantity application count differs for existing items (Quantity) vs gap-created items (Quantity-1).

key_file_guides.role: Inverse of apply: AddonRateCard.Restore per item, removes zero items, merges adjacent equal fragments.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Merge uses reflect.DeepEqual on Annotations and BillingBehaviorOverride — pointer fields must be DeepEqual-comparable.

key_file_guides.role: Predicate for whether a restored RateCard is effectively empty and safe to delete; inspects only Price and EntitlementTemplate fields.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Only measures fields AddonRateCard.Apply/Restore touches — adding other-field checks risks false positives.

key_file_guides.role: GetAffectedItemIDs computes map[rateCardKey][]itemID used by the HTTP layer for AffectedSubscriptionItemIds.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Has a FIXME noting it belongs elsewhere; do not add business logic here.

anti_patterns: Adding DB or service calls inside Diffable.GetApplies/GetRestores — this is a pure in-memory transform
Calling spec.Apply with a nil Diffable (GetDiffableFromAddon returns nil when no instances exist)
Using absolute timestamps in SubscriptionItemSpec cadence fields instead of ISODuration offsets relative to phase start
Modifying phase.ItemsByKey in-place — restore.go always rebuilds filteredItems and mergedItems slices
Assuming Apply then Restore is a no-op for all items — only items fully within the addon cadence with zero restored value are deleted

decisions.decision: Pure in-memory spec mutation with no I/O
decisions.rationale: diff/restore must be composable and testable without a DB; callers (workflow service) own the persistence boundary.

decisions.decision: Gap-based insertion rather than full spec rebuild
decisions.rationale: Preserves existing item splits from prior edits and only touches the time range covered by the addon cadence.

code_examples.scenario: Apply addon to spec and later restore it

openmeter/subscription/addon/http.purpose: HTTP handler layer for subscription addon CRUD, bridging the v1 API to subscriptionaddon.Service and subscriptionworkflow.Service via the standard httptransport.HandlerWithArgs[Request,Response,Params] pipeline.

patterns.name: HandlerWithArgs three-stage pipeline
patterns.description: Each operation uses httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs(decoder, operation, encoder, ...options): decoder resolves namespace and decodes body, operation calls domain services, encoder writes JSON with a fixed status.
patterns.example: httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs(decoderFn, operationFn, commonhttp.JSONResponseEncoderWithStatus[Resp](http.StatusOK), httptransport.AppendOptions(h.Options, httptransport.WithOperationName("op"))...)

patterns.name: Upsert-on-create convenience
patterns.description: CreateSubscriptionAddon checks if the addon is already attached and routes to ChangeAddonQuantity instead of AddAddon, giving idempotent create semantics; both paths return HTTP 200.
patterns.example: if sAdd, ok := lo.Find(subsAdds.Items, func(s subscriptionaddon.SubscriptionAddon) bool { return s.Addon.ID == req.AddonInput.AddonID }); ok { ... ChangeAddonQuantity } else { ... AddAddon }

patterns.name: Namespace resolved via NamespaceDecoder
patterns.description: All decoders call h.resolveNamespace(ctx), delegating to h.NamespaceDecoder.GetNamespace(ctx); a missing namespace returns HTTP 500 via commonhttp.NewHTTPError.
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return ..., err }

patterns.name: Response always requires SubscriptionView
patterns.description: MapSubscriptionAddonToResponse needs both the SubscriptionAddon and its parent SubscriptionView to compute AffectedSubscriptionItemIds via addondiff.GetAffectedItemIDs; every Get/List endpoint fetches the view separately.
patterns.example: view, err := h.SubscriptionService.GetView(ctx, req.SubscriptionID); return MapSubscriptionAddonToResponse(view, *res)

key_file_guides.role: Defines the Handler interface, HandlerConfig (three services + NamespaceDecoder + Logger), NewHandler, and resolveNamespace.
key_file_guides.watch_for: HandlerConfig embeds all three services (SubscriptionAddonService, SubscriptionWorkflowService, SubscriptionService) — all required; missing any panics at call time.

key_file_guides.role: MapSubscriptionAddonToResponse builds api.SubscriptionAddon including AffectedSubscriptionItemIds; MapCreateSubscriptionAddonRequestToInput translates API body to workflow input.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Errors if the addon has no instances (empty union of periods); calls addondiff.GetAffectedItemIDs which needs the full SubscriptionView.

key_file_guides.role: Upsert-on-create: if attached, ChangeAddonQuantity; else AddAddon. Both paths return HTTP 200.
key_file_guides.watch_for: No 201/200 distinction between create and update — intentional for idempotency.

key_file_guides.role: PATCH-style quantity update; requires both Timing and Quantity, else a 400 validation error.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Timing/Quantity nil-checks are manual in the decoder, not via model.Validate — keep them in sync with the API schema.

anti_patterns: Adding business logic to decoder functions — decoders only parse and validate shape
Forgetting to fetch SubscriptionView before MapSubscriptionAddonToResponse
Using NewHandler instead of NewHandlerWithArgs when URL path params are needed
Passing a nil SubscriptionView to MapSubscriptionAddonToResponse

decisions.rationale: Clients often want idempotent addon attachment; routing to ChangeAddonQuantity when already present avoids a 409 conflict and simplifies client logic.

code_examples.scenario: Standard handler wiring with URL params and namespace resolution

openmeter/subscription/addon/repo.purpose: Ent-backed PostgreSQL repository for subscription addons and their quantity timeline, implementing entutils.TransactingRepo so all mutations participate in caller-supplied transactions. Quantities are append-only.

patterns.name: TransactingRepo wrapping all mutations
patterns.description: Every method body wraps in entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, r, func(ctx, repo) ...) so the repo rebinds to the ctx transaction or starts its own via Self(). Never call repo.db directly in a method body.
patterns.example: return entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, r, func(ctx context.Context, repo *subscriptionAddonRepo) (*models.NamespacedID, error) { entity, err := repo.db.SubscriptionAddon.Create()...Save(ctx); return &models.NamespacedID{ID: entity.ID, Namespace: entity.Namespace}, err })

patterns.name: Tx/Self/WithTx transaction trinity
patterns.description: Each repo struct implements Tx() (HijackTx + NewTxDriver), Self() (identity), and WithTx() (rebind via NewTxClientFromRawConfig). Both subscriptionAddonRepo and subscriptionAddonQuantityRepo have their own block in transaction.go.
patterns.example: func (r *subscriptionAddonRepo) WithTx(ctx context.Context, tx *entutils.TxDriver) *subscriptionAddonRepo { txClient := db.NewTxClientFromRawConfig(ctx, *tx.GetConfig()); return NewSubscriptionAddonRepo(txClient.Client()) }

patterns.name: Eager-load via querySubscriptionAddon helper
patterns.description: All Get/List queries go through querySubscriptionAddon() which loads WithAddon (Ratecards->Features, Ratecards->TaxCode) and WithQuantities ordered by ActiveFrom asc, CreatedAt asc. Never use a bare db.SubscriptionAddon.Query().
patterns.example: query.WithAddon(func(aq *db.AddonQuery) { aq.WithRatecards(func(arq *db.AddonRateCardQuery) { arq.WithFeatures(); arq.WithTaxCode() }) }).WithQuantities(...)

patterns.name: Paginate-or-return-all for List
patterns.description: List returns all results when filter.Page.IsZero(); otherwise uses Ent's Paginate and entutils.MapPagedWithErr.
patterns.example: if filter.Page.IsZero() { entities, _ := query.All(ctx) } else { paged, _ := query.Paginate(ctx, filter.Page); return entutils.MapPagedWithErr(paged, MapSubscriptionAddon) }

patterns.name: Quantities are append-only
patterns.description: SubscriptionAddonQuantityRepository has only Create — no Update or Delete. The quantity timeline is an audit-safe ledger; changes are new records with a later ActiveFrom.
patterns.example: // Only Create is defined — no Update/Delete on subscriptionAddonQuantityRepo

key_file_guides.role: Create, Get, List for SubscriptionAddon; Get wraps db.IsNotFound in models.NewGenericNotFoundError.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All queries must use querySubscriptionAddon() — bare queries miss eager-loaded RateCard/Quantity edges.

key_file_guides.role: Append-only Create for SubscriptionAddonQuantity; namespace comes from parent subscriptionAddonID.Namespace.
key_file_guides.watch_for: No Update or Delete methods exist by design — do not add them.

key_file_guides.role: MapSubscriptionAddon(s) convert db rows to domain types, delegating RateCard mapping to addonrepo.FromAddonRateCardRow.
key_file_guides.watch_for: MapSubscriptionAddon errors if Edges.Addon is nil for rate card mapping — the edge must always be loaded via querySubscriptionAddon.

key_file_guides.role: Tx/Self/WithTx for both subscriptionAddonRepo and subscriptionAddonQuantityRepo.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Both repos use db.HijackTx independently — a third repo type needs its own Tx/Self/WithTx block.

anti_patterns: Calling repo.db methods directly inside Create/Get/List without TransactingRepo wrapping
Fetching SubscriptionAddon without the querySubscriptionAddon() helper — misses eager-loaded edges
Updating or deleting SubscriptionAddonQuantity rows — quantities are immutable append-only
Using a raw db.Client in WithTx instead of db.NewTxClientFromRawConfig

decisions.rationale: The quantity timeline is an audit-safe ledger; changes are new records with a later ActiveFrom rather than modifying existing rows.

code_examples.scenario: Create subscription addon inside a transaction

openmeter/subscription/addon/service.purpose: Business logic service for subscription addons: validates plan compatibility, instance-type constraints, and MaxQuantity limits before persisting via SubscriptionAddonRepository and publishing Watermill events. Implements subscriptionaddon.Service.

patterns.name: Config struct with Validate() and fail-fast construction
patterns.description: NewService accepts a Config and calls cfg.Validate() before returning — fail-fast at wiring time rather than runtime nil-pointer panics.
patterns.example: func NewService(cfg Config) (subscriptionaddon.Service, error) { if err := cfg.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }; return &service{cfg: cfg}, nil }

patterns.name: Pre-persist validation chain
patterns.description: Create and ChangeQuantity both (1) input.Validate(), (2) fetch the addon to check InstanceType, (3) fetch the subscription view for PlanRef and current phase, (4) fetch PlanAddon for FromPlanPhase and MaxQuantity — only then enter transaction.Run.
patterns.example: if err := input.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }; add, err := s.cfg.AddonService.GetAddon(ctx, ...); if add.InstanceType == productcatalog.AddonInstanceTypeSingle && input.InitialQuantity.Quantity != 1 { return nil, models.NewGenericValidationError(...) }

patterns.name: transaction.Run for multi-step writes
patterns.description: All writes use transaction.Run(ctx, s.cfg.TxManager, func) coordinating the repo Tx/Self/WithTx chain; repo calls inside the func use the tx-bound ctx.
patterns.example: return transaction.Run(ctx, s.cfg.TxManager, func(ctx context.Context) (*subscriptionaddon.SubscriptionAddon, error) { repo.Create(ctx, ns, ...); qtyRepo.Create(ctx, id, ...); add, _ := repo.Get(ctx, id); s.cfg.Publisher.Publish(ctx, subscriptionaddon.NewCreatedEvent(...)); return add, nil })

patterns.name: Event publish inside transaction
patterns.description: After persisting, Publisher.Publish runs inside the transaction.Run func so the event is tied to the same unit of work. NewCreatedEvent/NewChangeQuantityEvent are factories in the subscriptionaddon package.
patterns.example: s.cfg.Publisher.Publish(ctx, subscriptionaddon.NewCreatedEvent(ctx, sView.Customer, *subscriptionAddon))

patterns.name: Phase-ordered compatibility check
patterns.description: To verify an addon can be added at a time, the service iterates sView.Phases in order and checks whether compatibility.FromPlanPhase is reached before the phase containing the addon start time.
patterns.example: for _, phase := range sView.Phases { if phase.Key == compatibility.FromPlanPhase { break }; if phase.Key == phaseAtAddonStart.PhaseKey { return nil, models.NewGenericValidationError(...) } }

key_file_guides.role: Full subscriptionaddon.Service implementation: Create, Get, List, ChangeQuantity with all validation and transaction coordination.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Create and ChangeQuantity duplicate validation (InstanceType, FromPlanPhase, MaxQuantity) — keep them in sync. Addons cannot be added to custom subscriptions (no PlanRef).

anti_patterns: Calling SubAddRepo or SubAddQtyRepo outside transaction.Run for writes — partial writes occur on failure
Bypassing cfg.Validate() by constructing the service struct directly instead of via NewService
Publishing events outside the transaction.Run func — event and DB write become inconsistent on rollback
Adding addons to subscriptions without a PlanRef — the service rejects custom subscriptions

decisions.decision: Service validates plan compatibility before any DB write
decisions.rationale: Addon purchase must respect plan-level constraints (FromPlanPhase, MaxQuantity, InstanceType) that live in the product catalog, not the subscription tables.
The quantity timeline is append-only and models a different entity; a dedicated repo keeps quantity writes isolated and avoids loading all quantities on every mutation.

code_examples.scenario: Create a subscription addon with validation and transactional persistence
